pi_items.source: Standard Linux home directory convention for user 'ga'; setup_task.sh copies file to this path
Standard Linux home directory convention; network_requirements.txt (line 56) specifies PDF export to ~/Diagrams/enterprise_network.pdf

XML parsing of enterprise_network_starter.drawio shows 4 vertex elements (ISP-A, ISP-B, Border-RTR-01, Border-RTR-02). README claims '5 (4 devices + 1 annotation)' but no 5th shape found in actual XML.
XML parsing shows 1 diagram element with name='Enterprise Network' in enterprise_network_starter.drawio
network_requirements.txt contains 'CORE LAYER', 'DISTRIBUTION LAYER', and 'ACCESS LAYER' sections with detailed device specifications
network_requirements.txt line 47 states 'Create a SECOND diagram page named exactly "OOB Management"'; requirements also mention OOB Management network components

Requirements specify: 2 core switches + 4 distribution switches + 8 access switches + 2 OOB devices = 16 new shapes plus 4 existing shapes = 20 total. README Ground Truth states 'Expected final shapes: ≥20'

privileged_info_summary: The starting diagram contains 4 vertex shapes (2 ISP clouds + 2 border routers), not 5 as claimed in metadata. The diagram has 1 page initially and must expand to 2 pages (adding OOB Management page). The task requires adding three hierarchical network layers (core, distribution, access) with specific Cisco device models per the requirements document. The minimum shape requirement of 20 is achievable by adding 16 new devices (2 core + 4 distribution + 8 access + 2 OOB) to the existing 4, meeting the 20-shape threshold exactly. Color-coding scheme is documented in requirements (gold for WAN, blue for core, green for distribution, orange for access, purple for OOB). All inter-layer links must be labeled with bandwidth and protocol (e.g., '40Gbps / OSPF').
